project.name: Smart Thermostat Controller — Safety-Critical Firmware v1.0

work_products.title: Safety Plan
work_products.content: This safety plan defines the activities, responsibilities, and milestones for the thermostat controller safety lifecycle. The Safety Manager owns the plan. Gate reviews occur at concept, design, integration, and release. The plan is version-controlled and updated at each milestone.

work_products.title: Item Definition — Smart Thermostat
work_products.content: The smart thermostat controls HVAC output based on temperature sensor readings, user setpoints, and occupancy detection. Interfaces include temperature sensor (I2C), relay outputs (heating/cooling), Wi-Fi module, and user display. Operating range: 0C to 50C ambient. Operating modes: heating, cooling, standby, fault. Potential malfunctions include sensor failure leading to overheating, relay stuck-on, and loss of communication.

work_products.title: Hazard Analysis and Risk Assessment
work_products.content: HE1: Overheating due to sensor failure causing continuous heating (Severity: High, Exposure: High, Controllability: Medium → Level B). HE2: Cooling failure in extreme heat (Severity: Medium, Exposure: Medium, Controllability: High → Level A). Safety integrity levels assigned per the risk matrix.

work_products.title: Safety Goals
work_products.content: SG1: The thermostat shall prevent continuous heating when the ambient temperature exceeds the setpoint by more than 5 degrees (Level B). SG2: The thermostat shall enter a safe state (heating off) upon detection of sensor failure (Level B).

work_products.title: Technical Safety Concept
work_products.content: TSR1: Independent temperature watchdog shall trigger heating cutoff if temperature exceeds setpoint + 5C for more than 30 seconds. TSR2: Sensor plausibility check shall detect open-circuit and short-circuit faults within 2 seconds. TSR3: Safe state (all relays off) shall be entered within 500ms of any detected safety-relevant fault.
